pdg_node.informal_desc: Determines the global energy scale for the Hamiltonian by calculating the interaction strength for the shortest non-connected qubit pair, which defines the Rydberg blockade radius.

pdg_node.informal_desc: Constructs the adiabatic pulse sequence based on simulation parameters and register geometry. This includes defining the amplitude and detuning waveforms for the global Rydberg channel and configuring the local detuning map.

declaration.type_signature: (register: QuantumRegister, parameters: Dict) -> Sequence
declaration.docstring: Constructs the adiabatic pulse sequence based on simulation parameters and register geometry. This includes defining the amplitude and detuning waveforms for the global Rydberg channel and configuring the local detuning map.

pdg_node.statement: (raw_simulation_results: SimulationResults, permutation_list: List[Permutation], backend_flags: Dict) -> CountDistribution
pdg_node.informal_desc: Extracts final measurement counts from the raw simulation results by sampling the final state. For the MPS backend, it also applies an inverse permutation to the sampled bitstrings to align them with the original qubit order.

pdg_node.statement: (measurement_counts: CountDistribution, final_register: QuantumRegister, num_solutions: Integer) -> List[Solution]
pdg_node.informal_desc: Processes the raw results from the quantum sampler. It interprets the measurement count distribution to extract the top solutions for the optimization problem.

declaration.name: solutionextraction
declaration.type_signature: (measurement_counts: CountDistribution, final_register: QuantumRegister, num_solutions: Integer) -> List[Solution]
declaration.docstring: Processes the raw results from the quantum sampler. It interprets the measurement count distribution to extract the top solutions for the optimization problem.
declaration.conceptual_summary: MostProbableStateDecoder. Filters a discrete probability distribution to identify the 'K' most frequently observed states. It then decodes these high-frequency states from a raw format into a structured representation, effectively reducing a large outcome space to a small set of the most likely results.. Applications: Genomics: Identifying the most common genetic variants in a population from sequencing count data., Natural Language Processing: Extracting the most likely candidate words from a language model's output probability distribution for use in beam search decoding., Failure Analysis: Pinpointing the most frequent error codes from system logs to prioritize investigation of system failure modes., Market Research: Determining the most popular product choices from a large set of customer transaction data.
